translations.idiomatic: The regional commander was determined to have him and tricked the county magistrate into delivering him to the yamen gate, yet could not force him to serve.

sentences.zh: 均於是客於濮陽。

translations.literal: Jun thereupon sojourned as guest at Puyang.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Jun then withdrew to live as a guest in Puyang.

sentences.zh: 建初三年，司徒鮑昱辟之，後舉直言，並不詣。

translations.literal: In the third year of Jianchu, Minister of Education Bao Yu summoned him; later he was recommended for outspoken integrity; both times he did not go.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianchu 3 Minister Bao Yu called for him, and later he was nominated for blunt counsel; he answered neither summons.

sentences.zh: 六年，公車特征。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, the public carriage specially summoned him.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year the court sent the imperial coach for him alone.

sentences.zh: 再遷尚書，數納忠言，肅宗敬重之。

translations.literal: Twice transferred to Director of the Secretariat, he repeatedly submitted loyal words; Emperor Suzong respected him.
translations.idiomatic: Promoted twice to director of the secretariat, he spoke plainly and often; Emperor Zhang held him in high regard.

sentences.zh: 後以病乞骸骨，拜議郎，告歸，因稱病篤，帝賜以衣冠。

translations.literal: Later citing illness he begged for his bones, was appointed Gentleman Consultant, announced return home, thereupon claimed grave illness; the emperor bestowed on him cap and robes.
translations.idiomatic: Later he asked to retire, was made gentleman consultant, went home pleading mortal illness, and received cap and gown from the throne.

sentences.zh: 元和元年，詔告廬江太守、東平相曰：『議郎鄭均，束脩安貧，恭儉節整，前在機密，以病致仕，守善貞固，黃發不怠。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yuanhe, an edict instructed the grand administrator of Lujiang and the minister of Dongping, saying, 'Gentleman Consultant Zheng Jun, with bundle of dried meat is content in poverty, respectful, frugal, restrained, and orderly; earlier in secretarial posts he retired on illness, guards goodness and is chaste and firm, yellow-haired without slackening.'
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanhe 1 an edict told the governors of Lujiang and Dongping: 'Gentleman Consultant Zheng Jun lives frugally on a teacher's stipend, humble and orderly; he left confidential office for illness, keeps faith in old age, and never slackens.'

sentences.zh: 又前安邑令毛義，躬履遜讓，比征辭病，淳潔之風，東州稱仁。

translations.literal: Moreover, former chief of Anyi Mao Yi personally practiced yielding and modesty; compared to being summoned he declined illness; the wind of purity and simplicity, the eastern provinces praise as benevolent.'
translations.idiomatic: Former magistrate Mao Yi of Anyi chose modest retirement over repeated calls—his purity is hailed as humanity in the eastern provinces.'

sentences.zh: 書不雲乎：「章厥有常，吉哉！

translations.literal: Does not the Document say, "When good order has its constant rule, that is auspicious!"
translations.idiomatic: The Documents put it this way: "When a ruler's rule holds steady, blessing follows!"

sentences.zh: 」其賜均、義谷各千斛，常以八月長吏存問，賜羊酒，顯茲異行。

translations.literal: "Let each be granted a thousand hu of grain; constantly in the eighth month let the senior clerk inquire after them and bestow sheep and wine, displaying these unusual conducts.'
translations.idiomatic: Grant each a thousand hu of grain; each eighth month let local officials call on them with sheep and wine to mark their rare virtue.'

sentences.zh: 』明年，帝東巡過任城，乃幸均舍，敕賜尚書祿以終其身，故時人號為『白衣尚書』。

translations.literal: The next year, the emperor on eastern tour passed Rencheng, thereupon visited Jun's house, ordered bestowal of a Director of the Secretariat's salary for the rest of his life; therefore people of the time called him the "commoner Director of the Secretariat."'
translations.idiomatic: The next year, passing Rencheng on an eastern tour, the emperor called at Zheng Jun's home and granted him a director's salary for life—so men dubbed him the 'minister in plain clothes.'

sentences.zh: 永元中，卒於家。

translations.literal: In the Yongyuan period, he died at home.
translations.idiomatic: He died at home during the Yongyuan era.

sentences.zh: 趙典字仲經，蜀郡成都人也。

translations.literal: Zhao Dian, courtesy name Zhongjing, was a man of Chengdu in Shujun commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Dian, styled Zhongjing, was from Chengdu in Shu commandery.

sentences.zh: 父戒，為太尉，桓帝立，以定策封廚亭侯。

translations.literal: His father Jie was Grand Commandant; when Emperor Huan ascended, for planning the enthronement he was enfeoffed Marquis of Chuting.
translations.idiomatic: His father Zhao Jie was grand commandant and, for fixing Emperor Huan's succession, was enfeoffed as marquis of Chuting.

sentences.zh: 典少篤行隱約，博學經書，弟子自遠方至。

translations.literal: Dian in youth was earnest in conduct, retiring and modest, broadly learned in the classics and scriptures, and disciples came from distant regions.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Dian in youth was grave and retiring, wide-read in the classics, and drew students from afar.

sentences.zh: 建和初，四府表薦，征拜議郎，侍講禁內，再遷為侍中。

translations.literal: At the beginning of Jianhe, the four offices jointly memorialized recommending him; he was summoned and appointed Gentleman Consultant, lectured within the forbidden precincts, and was twice transferred to Palace Attendant.
translations.idiomatic: Early in Jianhe the chief bureaus recommended him together; he became gentleman consultant, lectured inside the palace, and rose twice to palace attendant.

sentences.zh: 時，帝欲廣開鴻池，典諫曰：『鴻池泛溉，已且百頃，猶復增而深之，非所以崇唐、虞之約己，遵孝之愛人也。

translations.literal: At that time the emperor wished broadly to open Hong Pool; Dian remonstrated, saying, 'Hong Pool in flooding and irrigation is already nearly a hundred qing; again to increase and deepen it is not the means by which to honor Tang and Yu's restraint upon themselves or to practice filial piety's love for the people.'
translations.idiomatic: When the emperor planned to enlarge Hong Pool, Zhao Dian urged: 'It already floods nearly a hundred qing; to dig it wider and deeper is no way to honor the self-restraint of Yao and Shun or to show the humane care expected of a filial ruler.'

sentences.zh: 』帝納其言而止。

translations.literal: The emperor accepted his words and stopped.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor took his point and dropped the project.

sentences.zh: 父卒，襲封。

translations.literal: When his father died, he inherited the fief.
translations.idiomatic: He succeeded to the title when his father died.

sentences.zh: 出為弘農太守，轉右扶風。

translations.literal: He went out as grand administrator of Hongnong, then transferred to right Administrator of Fufeng.
translations.idiomatic: He served as grand administrator of Hongnong, then as right administrator of Fufeng.

sentences.zh: 公事去官，征拜城門校尉，轉將作大匠，遷少府，又轉大鴻臚。

translations.literal: For public affairs he left office, was summoned and appointed Colonel of the City Gates, transferred to Court Architect, transferred to Privy Treasurer, again transferred to Grand Herald.
translations.idiomatic: He left office over an official matter, was recalled as colonel of the city gates, then court architect, privy treasurer, and finally grand herald.

sentences.zh: 時，恩澤諸侯以無勞受封，群臣不悅而莫敢諫，典獨奏曰：『夫無功而賞，勞者不勸，上忝下辱，亂象幹度。

translations.literal: At that time enfeoffed marquises of favor received fiefs without merit; the host of ministers was displeased yet none dared remonstrate; Dian alone submitted memorial, saying, 'When there is reward without merit, those who labor are not encouraged; above is shamed, below insulted, and disorderly signs interfere with the celestial norm.'
translations.idiomatic: When court favorites won marquisates without service, the ministers fumed in silence; Zhao Dian alone wrote, 'Reward without work discourages the deserving, shames the throne, insults the people, and lets disorder mock Heaven's pattern.'

sentences.zh: 且高祖之誓，非功臣不封。

translations.literal: Moreover Gaozu's oath was: unless a meritorious minister, do not enfeoff.'
translations.idiomatic: Moreover the founder swore that no man without merit should be enfeoffed.'

sentences.zh: 宜一切削免爵士，以存舊典。

translations.literal: It is fitting utterly to strip away titles and fiefs, in order to preserve the old norm.'
translations.idiomatic: Strip these sinecures away and restore the old standard.'

sentences.zh: 』帝不從。

translations.literal: The emperor did not follow.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor would not agree.

sentences.zh: 頃之，轉太仆，遷太常。

translations.literal: Before long, he transferred to Grand Coachman, transferred to Grand Master of Ceremonies.
translations.idiomatic: Soon he moved to grand coachman, then grand master of ceremonies.

sentences.zh: 朝廷每有災異疑議，輒咨問之。

translations.literal: Whenever the court had prodigies or doubtful points for deliberation, it always consulted and asked him.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever omens or disputed policy troubled the court, it turned to him for judgment.

sentences.zh: 典據經正對，無所曲折。

translations.literal: Dian according to the classics answered straight, without twisting.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Dian answered from the classics without trimming his words.

sentences.zh: 每得賞賜，輒分與諸生之貧者。

translations.literal: Whenever he obtained rewards and gifts, he always divided them among the students who were poor.
translations.idiomatic: Every imperial gift he shared with his neediest pupils.

sentences.zh: 後以諫爭違旨，免官就國。

translations.literal: Later for remonstrating and disputing contrary to imperial intent, he was dismissed from office and went to his state.
translations.idiomatic: He later lost office for opposing the throne too bluntly and retired to his fief.

sentences.zh: 會帝崩，時禁藩國諸侯不得奔吊，典慨然曰：『身從衣褐之中，致位上列。

translations.literal: When it happened that the emperor died, at that time it was forbidden for feudal princes of the kingdoms to hurry to mourning; Dian sighed with feeling, saying, 'My person from among coarse cloth has reached the upper ranks.'
translations.idiomatic: When the emperor died, princes were barred from the capital for mourning; Zhao Dian declared, 'I rose from common cloth to the highest ranks.'

sentences.zh: 且鳥烏反哺報德，況於士邪！

translations.literal: Moreover birds and crows in turning to feed repay virtue—how much more a gentleman!'
translations.idiomatic: Even crows feed their parents—should a scholar do less!'

sentences.zh: 』遂解印綬符策付縣，而馳到京師。

translations.literal: Thereupon he untied seal ribbons and tallies and handed them to the county, and galloped to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: He handed his seal and credentials to the county magistrate and galloped to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 州郡及大鴻臚並執處其罪，而公卿百寮嘉典之義，表請以租自贖，詔書許之。

translations.literal: Province, commandery, and Grand Herald together held him for his crime, but the dukes and ministers and hundred officials praised Dian's righteousness, memorialized asking to redeem with rent, and an edict permitted it.
translations.idiomatic: Province, commandery, and grand herald would have punished him, but the high ministers praised his devotion and memorialized to ransom his offense with grain, which the emperor allowed.

sentences.zh: 再遷長樂少府、衛尉。

translations.literal: Twice transferred to junior minister of Changle and Commandant of the Guards.
translations.idiomatic: He was twice promoted to junior minister of Changle and commandant of the guards.

sentences.zh: 公卿復表典篤學博聞，宜備國師。

translations.literal: The dukes and ministers again memorialized that Dian was earnest in learning and broadly heard, fitting to be prepared as state teacher.
translations.idiomatic: They again urged that Zhao Dian's learning fitted him to be tutor to the realm.

sentences.zh: 會病卒，使者吊祠。

translations.literal: It happened that he died of illness; envoys attended mourning sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: He died of illness first; the court sent envoys to his funeral.

sentences.zh: 竇太后復遣使兼贈印綬，謚曰獻侯。

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Dou again sent envoys concurrently to bestow seal ribbons, posthumous title Xian Marquis.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Dou sent envoys to confer his seals posthumously and named him Marquis Xian.

sentences.zh: 典兄子謙，謙弟溫，相繼為三公。

translations.literal: Dian's elder brother's son Qian, Qian's younger brother Wen, in succession were the Three Dukes.
translations.idiomatic: His nephews Zhao Qian and Zhao Wen each rose in turn to the three excellencies.

sentences.zh: 謙字產信，初平元年，代黃琬為太尉。

translations.literal: Qian, courtesy name Chanxin, in the first year of Chuping replaced Huang Wan as Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Qian, styled Chanxin, became grand commandant in Chuping 1, replacing Huang Wan.

sentences.zh: 獻帝遷都長安，以謙行車騎將軍，為前置。

translations.literal: When Emperor Xian moved the capital to Chang'an, he made Qian act as general of chariots and cavalry and vanguard.
translations.idiomatic: When the capital moved to Chang'an, the emperor named Zhao Qian acting general of chariots and cavalry to lead the train.

sentences.zh: 明年病罷。

translations.literal: The next year he was dismissed for illness.
translations.idiomatic: Illness forced him out the following year.

sentences.zh: 復為司隸校尉。

translations.literal: Again he became Metropolitan Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: He was again appointed metropolitan commandant.

sentences.zh: 車師王侍子為董卓所愛，數犯法，謙收殺之。

translations.literal: The hostage son of the king of Cheshi was favored by Dong Zhuo, repeatedly violated the law; Qian arrested and executed him.
translations.idiomatic: The Cheshi king's son, a hostage at court and Dong Zhuo's favorite, broke the law again and again until Zhao Qian seized him and put him to death.

sentences.zh: 卓大怒，殺都官從事，而素敬憚謙，故不加罪。

translations.literal: Zhuo was greatly angry and killed the clerk for capital officials in his train, but had always revered and feared Qian, therefore did not add punishment to him.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhuo flew into a rage and executed Zhao Qian's retainer, yet he still respected Zhao Qian too much to touch him.

sentences.zh: 轉為前將軍，遣擊白波賊，有功，封郫侯。

translations.literal: He was transferred to be Former General, was sent to attack the White Wave bandits, had merit, and was enfeoffed Marquis of Pi.
translations.idiomatic: He was named former general, crushed the White Wave rebels, earned distinction, and was enfeoffed as marquis of Pi.

sentences.zh: 李傕殺司徒王允，復代允為司徒。

translations.literal: Li Jue killed Minister of Education Wang Yun; Qian again replaced Yun as Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: When Li Jue murdered Minister Wang Yun, Zhao Qian stepped into Wang Yun's place as minister of education.

sentences.zh: 數月病免，拜尚書令。

translations.literal: Several months later he was dismissed for illness and appointed Director of the Secretariat.
translations.idiomatic: A few months later illness forced him out; the court then named him director of the secretariat.

sentences.zh: 是年卒，溢曰忠侯。

translations.literal: That year he died; his posthumous title was Loyal Marquis.
translations.idiomatic: He died the same year and was canonized as Marquis Zhong—'the Loyal.'

sentences.zh: 溫字子柔，初為京兆丞，嘆曰：『大丈夫當雄飛，安能雌伏！

translations.literal: Wen, courtesy name Zirou, at first was assistant governor of the metropolitan region; he sighed, saying, 'A great man ought to soar like the male phoenix—how can he brood like the hen!'
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Wen, styled Zirou, began as assistant governor of the capital region and swore, 'A true man should rise like a hawk, not hide like a mouse!'

sentences.zh: 』遂棄官去。

translations.literal: Thereupon he abandoned office and departed.
translations.idiomatic: He threw down his seal and walked away.

sentences.zh: 遭歲大饑，散家糧以振窮餓，所活萬餘人。

translations.literal: Encountering a year of great famine, he distributed the household's grain to relieve the starving and hungry; those he saved numbered more than ten thousand.
translations.idiomatic: When famine struck he opened his granaries to the starving and kept more than ten thousand souls alive.

sentences.zh: 獻帝西遷都，為侍中，同輿輦至長安，封江南亭侯，代楊彪為司空，免，頃之，復為司徒，錄尚書事。

translations.literal: When Emperor Xian moved the capital west, he became Palace Attendant; together they rode the imperial carriage to Chang'an; he was enfeoffed Marquis of Jiangnan pavilion; he replaced Yang Biao as Minister of Works, was dismissed; before long again was Minister of Education, recording Director of Secretariat affairs.
translations.idiomatic: He rode west with Emperor Xian as palace attendant, won the title marquis of Jiangnan precinct, succeeded Yang Biao as minister of works, briefly lost the post, then returned as minister of education with authority over the secretariat.

sentences.zh: 時，李傕與郭汜相攻，傕遂虜掠禁省，動帝幸北塢，外內隔絕。

translations.literal: At that time Li Jue and Guo Si attacked each other; Jue thereupon plundered the forbidden precincts, moved the emperor to take refuge at the north stockade, and inside and outside were cut off.
translations.idiomatic: When Li Jue and Guo Si turned on each other, Li Jue sacked the palace, marched the emperor to the northern redoubt, and sealed the court off from the world.

sentences.zh: 傕素疑溫不與己同，乃內溫於塢中，又欲移乘輿於黃白城。

translations.literal: Jue had long suspected Wen did not share his aims; he thereupon shut Wen inside the stockade and again wished to move the imperial carriage to Huangbai City.
translations.idiomatic: Li Jue had never trusted Zhao Wen; he locked him in the fortress and planned to drag the imperial train off to Huangbai.

sentences.zh: 溫與傕書曰：『公前托為董公報仇，然實屠陷王城，殺戮大臣，天下不可家見而戶說也。

translations.literal: Wen wrote to Jue, saying, 'Lord, you earlier claimed to avenge Lord Dong, yet in truth you butchered and trapped the royal capital and slaughtered great ministers—the realm cannot be shown house by house and explained door to door.'
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Wen wrote: 'You claimed to avenge Dong Zhuo, yet you gutted the capital and cut down the great ministers—no edict can whitewash that across every hearth.'

sentences.zh: 今與郭汜爭睚眥之隙，以成千鈞之仇，人在塗炭，各不聊生。

translations.literal: Now with Guo Si you contend over the grudge of a sidelong glance, thereby completing an enmity of a thousand jun; people are in mud and charcoal, each without means to live.
translations.idiomatic: Now you feud with Guo Si over a dirty look until the hatred weighs a thousand pounds, while the people burn in misery and barely survive.

sentences.zh: 曾不改悟，遂成禍亂。

translations.literal: You have not changed and awakened, and thereupon have become disaster and disorder.
translations.idiomatic: You refuse to repent, and chaos follows in your wake.

sentences.zh: 朝廷仍下明詔，欲令和解。

translations.literal: The court repeatedly issued clear edicts, wishing to make you reconcile.
translations.idiomatic: The court has issued one clear order after another urging peace between you.

sentences.zh: 上命不行，威澤日損。

translations.literal: Superior commands are not carried out; might and grace are daily diminished.
translations.idiomatic: When imperial commands go unheeded, the throne's authority withers by the day.

sentences.zh: 而復欲移轉乘輿，更幸非所，此誠老夫所不達也。

translations.literal: Yet again you wish to move and turn the imperial carriage and again favor a place that is not right—this is truly what this old man cannot fathom.
translations.idiomatic: Now you would haul the emperor off to yet another wrong refuge—an old minister like me cannot fathom such folly.

sentences.zh: 於《易》，一為過，再為涉，三而弗改，滅其頂，兇。

translations.literal: In the Changes, once is fault, twice is fording, thrice without change—'destroy his crown,' inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: The Book of Changes warns: one slip is error, a second time you ford deep water, a third without reform brings ruin on your own head.

sentences.zh: 不如早共和解，引軍還屯，上安萬乘，下全人民，豈不幸甚。

translations.literal: It would be better early together to reconcile, to draw the army back and encamp, above to pacify the ten thousand chariots, below to preserve the people—how could that not be greatly fortunate.
translations.idiomatic: Better make peace now, pull your troops back to camp, secure the sovereign above and spare the people below—nothing could bring greater good fortune.

sentences.zh: 』傕大怒，欲遣人殺溫。

translations.literal: Jue was greatly angry and wished to send men to kill Wen.
translations.idiomatic: Li Jue raged and ordered Zhao Wen killed.

sentences.zh: 李傕從弟應，溫故掾也，諫之數日，乃獲免。

translations.literal: Li Jue's cousin Ying, Wen's former clerk, remonstrated for several days, and then he obtained exemption.
translations.idiomatic: Li Ying, Li Jue's cousin and once Zhao Wen's clerk, argued for days until Li Jue relented.

sentences.zh: 溫從車駕都許。

translations.literal: Wen followed the imperial carriage to the capital at Xu.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Wen followed the court when it settled at Xu.

sentences.zh: 建安十三年，以辟司空曹操子丕為掾，操怒，奏溫辟臣子弟，選舉不實，免官。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year of Jianan, for having summoned Minister of Works Cao Cao's son Pi as clerk, Cao was angry and memorialized that Wen in summoning sons of ministers had selection and promotion untrue to fact, and he was dismissed from office.
translations.idiomatic: In Jian'an 13 he recruited Cao Pi as a clerk in the ministry of works; Cao Cao, furious, impeached him for nepotism in appointments and stripped him of office.

sentences.zh: 是歲卒，年七十二。

translations.literal: That year he died, aged seventy-two.
translations.idiomatic: He died the same year at seventy-two.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：宣、鄭、二王，奉身清方。

translations.literal: The encomium says: Xuan, Zheng, and the two Wangs held their persons pure and upright.
translations.idiomatic: Summing up: Xuan Bing, Zheng Jun, and the two Wangs kept their lives as straight as a line.

sentences.zh: 杜林據古，張湛矜莊。

translations.literal: Du Lin relied on antiquity; Zhang Zhan was stern and dignified.
translations.idiomatic: Du Lin stood on the old texts; Zhang Zhan wore dignity like armor.

sentences.zh: 典以義黜，宮由德揚。

translations.literal: Dian by righteousness was demoted; Gong by virtue was raised.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Dian fell for speaking truth; Cheng Gong rose on quiet merit.

sentences.zh: 大儀鵠發，見表憲王。

translations.literal: Dayi swan took flight, seen as a model for the Prince of Dongping.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Dayi spread his wings like a swan and became the mirror held up to Prince Xian of Dongping.

sentences.zh: 少卿誌仕，終乘高箱。

translations.literal: Shaoqing set his will on office, and in the end rode the high carriage.
translations.idiomatic: Guo Shaoqing vowed his way to rank and at last rode the tall chariot he had promised himself.
